mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns across all tested windows, or Rank IC remains negative over a 4-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 260.0, elevated inflation expectations force monetary tightening and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in a walk-forward validation framework.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in retail sales indicate weakening consumer spending that immediately reduces near-term corporate revenue forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative retail sales changes fail to predict negative 5-day returns during demand contraction phases, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in out-of-sample testing.
